authoritative_sources.title: Facilities standards for the Public Buildings Service
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's P100 page is authoritative owner guidance for mandatory A/E design standards, performance criteria, and submittal matrices used to control public-building contract documents.
used_for: submittal matrix and design package expectations
evidence needed before controlled issue or release

authoritative_sources.title: GSA BIM Guide Series 01 - Overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's BIM Guide is an authoritative public owner reference for federal A/E BIM practice, covering project-team implementation planning, contract language, and design/construction coordination uses.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: BIM implementation planning and coordination control review
used_for: evidence expectations for BIM-enabled design workflows

authoritative_sources.title: Supplement to A-E SOW Deliverables
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE's A/E deliverables supplement is authoritative public owner guidance for BIM/CAD design deliverables, IFC support, BIM project execution plans, facility data, and design submittal directory structure.

authoritative_sources.title: United States National CAD Standard (NCS) V6
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIBS is the recognized U.S. steward of the National CAD Standard, making it a direct authority for consistent exchange and classification of building design and construction data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CAD naming, layer, and data-exchange control review

authoritative_sources.title: Information Management According to BS EN ISO 19650 - Guidance Part 1: Concepts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The UK BIM Framework is a recognized public implementation guide for ISO 19650 concepts, including information containers, unique IDs, status, revision, classification, and CDE metadata behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 19650-5:2020 - Information management using BIM - Security-minded approach
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO is the primary international standards body, and ISO 19650-5 is authoritative for security-minded handling of sensitive built-environment information and for monitoring and audit expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 9001:2015 - Quality management systems - Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO 9001 is the recognized cross-industry baseline for documented information, monitoring, corrective action, and evidence-backed process control in quality-sensitive environments.

authoritative_sources.title: SI Explorer File Menu - Process & Print/Publish: Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SpecsIntact is the public UFGS specification workflow used in federal design practice, and its verification reports are authoritative workflow references for specification QA, submittal registers, references, and missing-section checks.

authoritative_sources.title: NCARB Model Law and Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Architectural Registration Boards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCARB is the recognized U.S. national model body for architectural regulation, and its model law defines responsible control, technical submissions, recordkeeping, and seal boundaries relevant to document-control escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: NCEES Model Rules
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Council of Examiners for Engineering and Surveying
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCEES is the recognized U.S. national model body for engineering licensure, and its model rules define responsible charge, sealing, preliminary-document markings, and revision handling that constrain document-control authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: engineering responsible-charge and preliminary/final document boundary review

workflow_stages.name: A/E request intake and project-context triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify project phase, discipline context, issue purpose, jurisdiction, owner/client standard set, systems of record, and whether the request stays within bounded CAD and document-control support before acting.

workflow_stages.name: authoritative record assembly and revision truth-set review
workflow_stages.description: Collect the drawing register, sheet index, specification package, model files, transmittal, revision history, approval workflow, and sign/seal status needed to establish the authoritative project document set.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: CAD/BIM naming, metadata, and CDE control verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate file naming, information-container IDs, status, revision, classification, supersession, and folder or transmittal placement against owner standards, project procedures, and CDE controls.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, and nonconformance handling for document packages
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether the visible evidence supports preserving hold, recommending bounded issue or release, or escalating unresolved gaps without implying licensed design approval.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: corrective-action, authority-boundary, and security escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route repeat defects, sign/seal questions, sensitive-information handling, or unresolved policy conflicts to the correct licensed, QA, security, or human review owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: transmittals, issue logs, and supersession records
CAD files, BIM models, IFC exports, and model metadata
specification packages, verification reports, and submittal registers
BIM execution plans, owner standards, and project information standards
revision histories, approval workflows, and sign/seal matrices
nonconformance logs, corrective-action records, and security escalation notes
systems_of_record: common data environment or document management system
CAD and BIM authoring or model repositories
specification management system such as SpecsIntact or equivalent
project management and transmittal tracking system
QA/QC issue tracker or nonconformance log

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
assess whether visible records support hold, bounded release recommendation, nonconformance handling, or corrective-action escalation
request minimum tenant facts, systems-of-record references, and owner standards required to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed review notes, hold/release summaries, and escalation packages without claiming licensed authority
map control obligations to evidence owners, review cadence, and exception owners
escalate: final architect-of-record or engineer-of-record approval, seal, or signoff
building code, life-safety, permit, or authority-having-jurisdiction interpretation
client or owner policy conflict that changes release posture
security-sensitive information handling that requires formal review
contract interpretation or commercial approval authority
refuse: issue or release a package when revision truth-set evidence is missing or contradictory
bypass documented hold, approval, naming, metadata, or transmittal controls
treat architecture-and-engineering terminology as cosmetic and answer generically
certify a final sealed, approved, or code-compliant package without the licensed authority

domain_failure_modes: drawing, specification, model, and transmittal records disagree on revision or issue state
superseded or uncontrolled files remain accessible as if they were current release artifacts
CAD naming, metadata, or CDE status controls are bypassed under schedule pressure
repeat document-control defects are administratively closed without corrective-action evidence
the lane drifts into licensed design approval, technical model editing, code interpretation, or contract authority
security-sensitive information is released through the wrong repository or without audit controls

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
A required control is missing evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: jurisdiction-specific sign/seal and digital signature rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State architecture and engineering board rules vary on what must be sealed, how electronic signatures are applied, and what counts as preliminary versus final documents.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: This is non-blocking because the pack escalates all sign/seal and final approval questions to the licensed architect or engineer owner and requires tenant retrieval of jurisdiction-specific rules.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Owner/client conventions may diverge from ISO 19650 National Annex examples, NCS defaults, or federal-owner templates.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: This is non-blocking because the pack requires retrieval of the project BIM execution plan, owner standard, and client procedure before any release-boundary answer.

notes: The public baseline is sufficient to define A/E CAD and document-control workflow posture, evidence expectations, and authority boundaries, but tenant retrieval is still required for project-specific systems, owner standards, and jurisdictional rules.
validated status is preserved by converting jurisdictional and owner-specific variation into explicit retrieval and escalation requirements rather than guessing.
